page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is an Easv7-series virtual machine configured with 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture, it features 48 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, and AVX-512 BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA-NI. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple vectors. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as PassMark CPU and memory evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes both ranking in the top 10%. Database operations, measured via Redis SET and PassMark database tests, also place in the top 10%, with Redis reaching 139.15 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds for small and medium models rank in the top tier, though performance drops to average for large 70B model prompt processing.

This instance does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, requiring external disk attachment for persistent data. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a dense resource profile suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of high-frequency AMD EPYC cores and 768.0 GB of RAM makes the E96as_v7 highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance leverages 48 physical cores and supports advanced vector extensions including AVX-512 and AVX-512 BF16. It does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results position the E96as_v7 in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 139.15 million operations per second. The 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio offers a highly resource-dense profile for enterprise workloads. This server is optimized for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and data analytics pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 CPU. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory performance for large-scale databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, designed for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 virtual cores and 768 GB of RAM for demanding enterprise database and analytics workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
